proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_BRAF by feature_009.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` averaged -0.804698 in `feature_009 == 1` lines (n=5574) versus -0.249503 in `feature_009 == 0` lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_EGFR by feature_022.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` averaged -0.601046 in `feature_022 == 1` lines (n=3880) versus -0.252407 in `feature_022 == 0` lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_ERBB2 by feature_024.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_ERBB2` averaged -0.685595 in `feature_024 == 1` lines (n=4890) versus -0.253473 in `feature_024 == 0` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_KRAS by feature_014.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` averaged -0.559415 in `feature_014 == 1` lines (n=11842) versus -0.251959 in `feature_014 == 0` lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_RIT1 by feature_027.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` averaged -0.352716 in `feature_027 == 1` lines (n=4560) versus -0.257196 in `feature_027 == 0` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with high `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.819`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines below this threshold.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_KIF18A in colorectal versus other lineages.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.720354 in `feature_011 == "colorectal"` lines (n=5816) versus -0.252397 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_KIF18A by upper-quartile feature_028.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.469402 in `feature_028 >= 0.819` lines (n=12508) versus -0.252593 in lower-`feature_028` lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with both `feature_011 == "colorectal"` and high `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.819`) have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

The `dependency_KIF18A` effect is an interaction: neither non-colorectal high-`feature_028` lines nor colorectal low-`feature_028` lines show the strong negative dependency seen in `feature_011 == "colorectal"` and `feature_028 >= 0.819` lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test for the conjunction feature_011 == colorectal and feature_028 >= 0.819.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -0.928802 in colorectal/high-`feature_028` lines (n=4006) versus -0.252657 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-by-two interaction contrast over colorectal status and upper-quartile feature_028.
analyses.result_summary: Cell means were -0.252267 for non-colorectal/low-`feature_028`, -0.252941 for non-colorectal/high-`feature_028`, -0.259003 for colorectal/low-`feature_028`, and -0.928802 for colorectal/high-`feature_028`; the interaction contrast was strongly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within colorectal high-`feature_028` cell lines, `feature_035 == 1` enables the strongest `dependency_KIF18A` signal, so `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_028 >= 0.819`, and `feature_035 == 1` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test for feature_011 == colorectal, feature_028 >= 0.819, and feature_035 == 1.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KIF18A` averaged -1.111866 in colorectal/high-`feature_028`/`feature_035 == 1` lines (n=3133) versus -0.253014 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Conditional comparison within colorectal high-feature_028 lines by feature_035.
analyses.result_summary: Among colorectal high-`feature_028` lines, `feature_035 == 1` lines averaged -1.111866 for `dependency_KIF18A` (n=3133), while `feature_035 == 0` lines averaged -0.271825 (n=873).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than cell lines below this threshold.
Cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines below this threshold.
Cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than cell lines below this threshold.
Cell lines with high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 0.428`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines below this threshold.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_TMED10 by upper-quartile feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_TMED10` averaged -0.281605 in high-`feature_016` lines (n=12577) versus -0.244729 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_DHX9 by upper-quartile feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` averaged -0.361423 in high-`feature_016` lines (n=12577) versus -0.324339 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_POLR2A by upper-quartile feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_POLR2A` averaged -0.497174 in high-`feature_016` lines (n=12577) versus -0.392828 in other lines.

analyses.code: Welch difference-in-means test comparing dependency_RPL11 by upper-quartile feature_003.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` averaged -0.343967 in high-`feature_003` lines (n=12501) versus -0.245214 in other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_009 == 1` and `feature_011 == "skin"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than all other cell lines.
`feature_022 == 1` and `feature_011 == "brain"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than all other cell lines.
`feature_024 == 1` and `feature_011 == "breast"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than all other cell lines.
`feature_014 == 1` and `feature_011 == "pancreatic"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_BRAF.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_BRAF` averaged -0.811388 in `feature_009 == 1`/skin lines (n=1966) versus -0.290932 in all other lines; this did not improve on the broader `feature_009 == 1` main-effect split.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_EGFR.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_EGFR` averaged -0.611669 in `feature_022 == 1`/brain lines (n=815) versus -0.273957 in all other lines; the broader `feature_022 == 1` split remained the cleaner driver signal.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_ERBB2.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_ERBB2` averaged -0.702714 in `feature_024 == 1`/breast lines (n=1121) versus -0.286401 in all other lines; the broader `feature_024 == 1` split remained stronger overall.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_KRAS.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_KRAS` averaged -0.559098 in `feature_014 == 1`/pancreatic lines (n=2913) versus -0.310281 in all other lines; this was significant but did not outperform the broader `feature_014 == 1` split.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_027 == 1` and `feature_011 == "brain"` cell lines have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other cell lines.
`feature_027 == 1` and low `feature_023` (`feature_023 <= 4.230`) cell lines have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_016 >= 0.964` and `feature_010 == "luminal"` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other lines.
Cell lines with `feature_016 >= 0.964` and high `feature_023` (`feature_023 >= 5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_RIT1.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` averaged -0.404687 in `feature_027 == 1`/brain lines (n=329) versus -0.264988 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_RIT1 using low feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RIT1` averaged -0.376141 in `feature_027 == 1`/low-`feature_023` lines (n=1145) versus -0.263323 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_TMED10.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_TMED10` averaged -0.296695 in high-`feature_016`/luminal lines (n=981) versus -0.253150 in all other lines.

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_DHX9` averaged -0.389195 in high-`feature_016`/high-`feature_023` lines (n=3320) versus -0.329718 in all other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_POLR2A`, the best-supported dependency subgroup is the broad high-`feature_016` group (`feature_016 >= 0.964`), with no two-feature subgroup improving meaningfully on this main effect.
Cell lines with high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 0.428`) and high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other lines.

analyses.code: Systematic predicate and two-predicate subgroup screen for dependency_POLR2A.
analyses.result_summary: The strongest interpretable `dependency_POLR2A` split was high `feature_016`: mean -0.497174 in the subgroup versus -0.392828 outside. Pair screens such as high `feature_016`/high `feature_023` were still significant but had smaller contrast (-0.085567).

analyses.code: Two-predicate subgroup screen result for dependency_RPL11.
analyses.result_summary: `dependency_RPL11` averaged -0.376555 in high-`feature_003`/high-`feature_016` lines (n=3630) versus -0.261555 in all other lines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported `dependency_BRAF` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than `feature_009 == 0` lines.
Final best-supported `dependency_EGFR` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than `feature_022 == 0` lines.
Final best-supported `dependency_ERBB2` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than `feature_024 == 0` lines.
Final best-supported `dependency_KRAS` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than `feature_014 == 0` lines.
Final best-supported `dependency_RIT1` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` and low `feature_023` (`feature_023 <= 4.230`) have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than all other lines.

Final best-supported `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup: cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, high `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.819`), and `feature_035 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other lines.

Final best-supported `dependency_TMED10` subgroup: cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) and `feature_010 == "luminal"` have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other lines.

Final best-supported `dependency_DHX9` subgroup: cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) and high `feature_023` (`feature_023 >= 5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than all other lines.
Final best-supported `dependency_POLR2A` subgroup: cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other lines.

Final best-supported `dependency_RPL11` subgroup: cell lines with high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 0.428`) and high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other lines.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_BRAF.
analyses.result_summary: Across the systematic single- and two-predicate search, `feature_009 == 1` was the cleanest `dependency_BRAF` subgroup; adding lineage predicates did not improve the overall contrast.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_EGFR.
analyses.result_summary: Across the systematic single- and two-predicate search, `feature_022 == 1` was the cleanest `dependency_EGFR` subgroup.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_ERBB2.
analyses.result_summary: Across the systematic single- and two-predicate search, `feature_024 == 1` was the cleanest `dependency_ERBB2` subgroup.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_KRAS.
analyses.result_summary: Across the systematic single- and two-predicate search, `feature_014 == 1` was the cleanest `dependency_KRAS` subgroup.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_RIT1.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1` was the main `dependency_RIT1` signal, with low `feature_023` defining a larger refined subgroup than the small brain-lineage subgroup.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_KIF18A.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_028 >= 0.819`, and `feature_035 == 1` defined the strongest `dependency_KIF18A` subgroup found.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_TMED10.
analyses.result_summary: The `dependency_TMED10` signal was modest but concentrated in high-`feature_016`/luminal lines.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_DHX9.
analyses.result_summary: The `dependency_DHX9` signal was strongest for high-`feature_016`/high-`feature_023` lines.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_POLR2A.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964` was the strongest broad `dependency_POLR2A` subgroup, and pairwise refinements did not improve meaningfully.

analyses.code: Final systematic heterogeneity screen summary for dependency_RPL11.
analyses.result_summary: `feature_003 >= 0.428` combined with `feature_016 >= 0.964` defined the strongest interpretable `dependency_RPL11` subgroup.
